name: Goga
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as combining geometric sans and neo-grotesque influences, citing Avenir and Helvetica as inspirations; sits squarely in the grotesque category with geometric leanings.
summary: A nine-weight grotesque family blending geometric and neo-grotesque influences, named after its distinctive alternate lowercase g forms.
description: Goga draws on Avenir and Helvetica to produce a neutral, large x-height grotesque suited to both display and text setting. Nine weights from Thin to Black come with matching italics, and the family ships as a variable font with axes for weight and slant. The stylistic alternate lowercase g forms are the one point of character in an otherwise general-purpose offering.
foundry_blurb: Narrow Type describe Goga as a versatile sans serif combining the best of geometric sans serifs and neo-grotesques, inspired by Avenir and Helvetica. Despite its universal, neutral character it carries a warm, friendly spirit, with unusual stylistic alternates for the lowercase g adding personality. The family supports Latin-extended languages, includes a broad set of OpenType features, and is available as a variable font with slant and weight axes.

name: Emilio
classification_notes: Emilio draws from old-style serif models such as Times and Garamond, with calligraphic details and a high x-height that place it in the humanist old-style tradition, updated with reductive modern elements.
summary: A 14-style old-style serif with calligraphic details and an eighties advertising flavour, suited to editorial and branding work.
description: Taking cues from the advertising serifs of the 1980s, particularly Times and Garamond, Emilio introduces modern touches such as reductive calligraphic stroke endings and distinctive K and R leg forms. Seven weights from Thin to Black each carry a matching italic, and a variable font is included in the bundle. A large x-height and modest contrast keep it readable at small sizes, while the display weights are the clearest expression of its character.
foundry_blurb: Narrow Type describe Emilio as a modern serif family of 14 styles that balances traditional and modern approaches. Inspired by eighties advertising visuals and typefaces like Times and Garamond, it introduces contemporary details including calligraphy-inspired reductive strokes and distinctive leg shapes on the K and R. Stylistic alternates, standard and discretionary ligatures, and other OpenType features are included, with the foundry positioning it for editorial design, posters, covers, branding and logo design.

name: Remixa
classification_notes: A modern sans-serif with serif-inspired detailing and high contrast, positioning it between a pure grotesque and a glyphic display face. The large x-height and display orientation reinforce its contemporary sans classification.
summary: A high-contrast display sans with subtle serif-derived details across six weights, aimed at branding and editorial use.
description: Six weights from Light to Black give Remixa a broad tonal range for headline and display work. Serif-inspired details and visible stroke contrast distinguish it from a plain grotesque, while a generous x-height keeps it legible at large sizes. OpenType features include standard and discretionary ligatures and stylistic alternates, making it a reasonable choice for magazine layouts, brand identities and logo work.
foundry_blurb: Narrow Type describe Remixa as a modern sans-serif that incorporates serif-inspired elements to create a contrasting display family. Six weights from Light to Black support versatile use across branding, logo design, editorial and magazine projects, with OpenType features including ligatures and stylistic alternates throughout.

name: Goga
classification_notes: Goga blends geometric sans construction with neo-grotesque neutrality, citing Avenir and Helvetica as inspirations, placing it in a hybrid geometric-grotesque subcategory.
summary: A nine-weight geometric grotesque that blends Avenir-style warmth with Helvetica-like neutrality, distinguished by playful alternate forms of the lowercase g.
description: Eighteen styles span nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic, and the family is also available as a variable font with axes for weight and slant. A large x-height aids legibility at smaller sizes, and the character set covers most Latin-script languages alongside OpenType features including old-style figures, tabular figures, fractions and circled numbers. The alternate lowercase g forms give it a mild personality that separates it from more anonymous grotesques in branding and editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Narrow Type describe Goga as a versatile sans serif combining the best of geometric sans serifs and neo-grotesques, inspired by Avenir and Helvetica. They position it as a universal yet warm and friendly grotesque, with unusual stylistic alternates for the lowercase g as its signature detail, suited to branding, editorial, apps, print and social media. It supports Latin-extended languages and is offered in variable font format with variable weight and slant axes.
